articles.title: Using Howdy on Linux like Windows Hello
articles.description: I use a Logitech Brio webcam, which has an infrared camera and works quite nicely with Windows Hello. On Linux, the closest equivalent I have found is Howdy, which lets you use face authentication via PAM.

articles.title: Under the Hood: How 2FA TOTP Authenticator Apps Work
articles.description: In the previous article, I walked through how TwoFac is structured as a Kotlin Multiplatform project. Now that the architecture is in place, it's time to zoom into one of the most important pieces inside.

articles.title: Architecting TwoFac: My Journey into Kotlin Multiplatform Module Structure
articles.description: In my previous post, I talked about why I'm building TwoFac. The short version? I got tired of proprietary digital cages like Authy and wanted an authenticator that was open, secure, and cross-platform.

articles.title: How Personal AI Agents and Agent Orchestrators like OpenClaw or GasTown are Made
articles.description: Over the last few months, projects like Gas Town by Steve Yegge and OpenClaw by Peter Steinberger have made AI agent orchestrators feel suddenly mainstream.

articles.title: env.sync.local - Syncing API keys and secrets between devices in my home LAN
articles.description: My desk is a heterogeneous graveyard of differing architectures. I have a MacBook Pro for daily work, a Razer laptop for Windows testing, and a Beelink mini-PC.

articles.title: Running llama.cpp (compiled from source) on AMD Strix Halo 395
articles.description: A quick doc/note/tutorial for referencing myself later. Here's how to get llama.cpp running with Vulkan support on AMD AI Max 395 (Strix Halo) based devices.

articles.title: sideproject diaries: sharetime.zone - A Simple Timezone Sharing Tool

articles.title: Beyond Copilot, Cursor and Claude Code: The Unbundled Coding AI Tools Stack
articles.description: The AI coding landscape has shifted. We went from just pay $20 for GitHub Copilot to a completely unbundled stack where you mix and match your Provider, Model, and Tool.

articles.title: The AI Revolution: Following the Path of Microchips and Cloud Computing
articles.description: The artificial intelligence revolution unfolding today mirrors two previous technological waves that fundamentally transformed how we work and create.

articles.title: My Next Project: Building the Open-Source, Cross-Platform Authenticator I Always Wanted
articles.description: Like many of you, I have a workflow. It's a finely tuned set of tools and habits that lets me move between my different machines—a Mac for primary development, a Windows machine for gaming and testing.

articles.description: I am sure a lot of you would have seen this particular meme template. It has given rise to entire genre of tiktoks where girls are amazed at how much calculation guys do to pick which stall to go to.

articles.title: Making one Jest test file depend on outputs from another
articles.description: I'll start off by saying this is an ugly hack, and I hope if you have reached this article, you really know what you're doing. Tests should be independent, and ideally not block each other.

articles.title: Understanding the shift of Frontend Development towards Declarative UI and redux-like state management
articles.description: Declarative UI and one-way flow of state is something that invariably developers seem to prefer and all frontend engineering platforms have been progressively moving towards it.

articles.title: Asking ChatGPT to build a YouTube Download App for me.
articles.description: So I have started seeing lots of people starting to publish videos where they ask generative AI to generate the code for an entire app and fairly complex apps are getting generated.

articles.description: So I recently made a Parking Lot CLI program (the kind of ones you make for low-level system design interview questions). The interesting thing is, it is made using Kotlin Multiplatform.

articles.title: Validating Github Actions Workflow files in Jetbrains IDEs
articles.description: If you maintain projects on Github, you must have come across Github Actions - which is Github's own CI provider where you can create workflow files in .github/workflow/<xyz>.yml.

articles.title: Creating and Publishing Visual Studio Code Color Themes
articles.description: Visual Studio Code has this in-built color theme called Quiet Light which I absolutely love, as I prefer using mostly light themes when coding in the day, when my room is filled with light.

articles.title: Managing libraries and dependencies in Android projects with Gradle version catalog
articles.description: In large multi-module Android (or for that matter any gradle-based) project, version management starts to become a bit of a pain. The most common way people have been coping with that was using buildSrc, but the problem with that is buildSrc changes...
